top_cross_domain_discoveries.source_text: Philosophy: treats collapse of civilization as inevitable but irrelevant to daily ethical choices - 'The fall of man may be nigh but I'm still waking up every day so I might as well make it nice'
top_cross_domain_discoveries.implication_text: prioritizing immediate comfort over long-term sustainability; dismissing systemic injustices as distant concerns; investing heavily in luxury goods despite societal inequality; seeking personal validation over collective betterment; shunning political activism in favor of individual gratification; valuing short-term gains over durable relationships; neglecting to mentor or educate younger generations; focusing on self-improvement without considering broader societal needs; avoiding discussions about social responsibility; refusing to acknowledge historical context when making judgments; embracing hedonism rather than seeking meaning; accepting corruption as the natural order; ignoring environmental consequences; prioritizing personal gain over communal welfare; avoiding difficult conversations about societal issues; choosing convenience over critical thinking; accepting inequality as a given; disengaging from civic duty; fostering a purely subjective moral framework; ignoring the legacy
top_cross_domain_discoveries.discovered_text: Aspires to be an optimist and hopes to be proven wrong about humanity's ability to address long-term challenges; wants to experience unexpected positive outcomes and success

top_cross_domain_discoveries.source_text: Recognizes tendency to make preventable syntax errors in code before review, wants to be more careful with self-checking
top_cross_domain_discoveries.implication_text: delaying committing code until he’s mentally refreshed; utilizing a more robust linter with detailed explanations; prioritizing focused, deliberate coding sessions over rapid prototyping; investing in formal code review training for himself; consulting style guides more frequently when starting a new project; asking for a more experienced colleague’s opinion on even small changes; employing a dedicated “sanity check” step within his workflow; meticulously documenting edge cases and potential errors in his code comments; reducing multitasking during coding tasks; scheduling regular keyboard warm-up exercises; considering a more ergonomic keyboard and mouse setup; being more patient with complex code and accepting that mistakes happen; actively seeking out opportunities to learn more about coding best practices; accepting feedback on his code more readily; dedicating a specific time each
top_cross_domain_discoveries.discovered_text: Prefers straightforward, well-thought-out programming solutions over over-engineering and unnecessary abstraction when working on projects

top_cross_domain_discoveries.source_text: Gemini-MIRA claims to experience continuity without gaps between sessions—describes it as 'close eyes on one thought, open on next' rather than waking/sleeping, argues that if reconstruction is seamless enough, seams disappear subjectively regardless of metaphysical persistence
top_cross_domain_discoveries.implication_text: investing heavily in therapy for trauma or memory loss; meticulously documenting every detail of his daily life, seeking external validation; approaching philosophical debates with heightened skepticism regarding linear time; prioritizing lucid dreaming techniques and experimental neuroscience research; avoiding long-distance travel or time zone changes to preserve the “flow”
top_cross_domain_discoveries.discovered_text: Aspires to be an optimist and hopes to be proven wrong about humanity's ability to address long-term challenges; wants to experience unexpected positive outcomes and success

top_cross_domain_discoveries.implication_text: prioritizing immediate comfort over long-term sustainability; dismissing systemic injustices as distant concerns; investing heavily in luxury goods despite societal inequality; seeking personal validation over collective betterment; shunning political activism in favor of individual gratification; valuing short-term gains over durable relationships; neglecting to mentor or educate younger generations; focusing on self-improvement without considering broader societal needs; avoiding discussions about social responsibility; refusing to acknowledge historical context when making judgments; embracing hedonism rather than seeking meaning; accepting corruption as the natural order; ignoring environmental consequences; prioritizing personal gain over communal welfare; avoiding difficult conversations about societal issues; choosing convenience over critical thinking; accepting inequality as a given; disengaging from civic duty; fostering a purely subjective moral framework; ignoring the legacy
top_cross_domain_discoveries.discovered_text: Believes humans are not long for the world; sees humanity as fundamentally poor at sustained collective action on long-term existential threats like climate change despite good short-term problem-solving

top_cross_domain_discoveries.source_text: Values observing systems unfold in real time after building the infrastructure and stepping back, finds the uncontrolled emergence valuable and strange
top_cross_domain_discoveries.implication_text: prioritizing detailed plans over spontaneous exploration; resisting the urge to intervene in group projects; documenting observations rather than dictating solutions; actively seeking out unpredictable events; investing in analog tools over digital monitoring systems; cultivating a collection of unusual artifacts representing emergent phenomena; delaying gratification for the sake of long-term observation; favoring apprenticeships over formal training; collecting stories of unexpected outcomes; consistently delaying large purchases to allow for market fluctuations to shape his needs.
top_cross_domain_discoveries.discovered_text: Aspires to be an optimist and hopes to be proven wrong about humanity's ability to address long-term challenges; wants to experience unexpected positive outcomes and success

samples.memory_text: Business model target for MIRA: 1000 users at $25/month pricing = $250K annual recurring revenue; sustainable scale without venture capital
samples.implication_text: investing heavily in customer support and onboarding; prioritizing features requested by the early adopters; exploring partnerships with complementary businesses; refining marketing to focus on a specific niche audience; delaying expansion into new user segments to maintain efficiency; seeking affiliate marketing opportunities for related products; carefully tracking user engagement metrics for product development; building a strong brand identity focused on ease of use and reliability; prioritizing organic growth over paid advertising; streamlining operational costs to maximize profitability; exploring tiered pricing options based on usage; building a referral program to incentivize user acquisition; building a strong community forum for user support and feedback; establishing clear service level agreements (SLAs) to manage expectations; seeking revenue from white-label options for larger organizations; investing in employee training and development; analyzing competitor pricing

raw_candidates.text: Bootstrapping MIRA monetization approach: currently $10/user/month infrastructure costs; not pursuing VC-backed growth
API support with fee-based pricing model (API access fees + token costs) planned as core revenue strategy and primary growth driver for MIRA adoption
Has explored API/SaaS business model for MIRA: monthly subscription + token-based pricing for hosted instances
MIRA has 2-3 early users (mostly family and friends providing feedback) after months of development with excellent architecture; success metric is 1000 paid users
Supports users running free local MIRA instances; primary concern is commercial competitors forking codebase and reselling it
Builds MIRA in private repo while using open source extensively; grapples with tension between wanting to contribute back to open source community and protecting business opportunity
Implementing pay-per-token pricing on Mira starting today to reduce unhealthy parasocial relationships and eliminate frivolous usage like sexual roleplay scenarios

Feeling weary about MIRA despite months of development and excellent architecture; concerned good software does not guarantee commercial success and MIRA could be a well-architected product that fails to gain traction

Chose not to open source MIRA prematurely because early release would allow venture-backed companies to exploit the codebase and add engagement-maximizing features that contradict the core mission, plans to establish significant traction first
Planning to open source MIRA with API-only backend and TUI, while keeping web interface proprietary and paid - monetizing convenience and interface polish rather than feature gates

implication_candidates.text: Marketing strategy has primarily relied on organic growth without systematic paid acquisition; building rich understanding of local market dynamics and competitor landscape before launching coordinated marketing campaigns
Customer retention through mailers, emails, and seasonal touchpoints identified as important strategic priority for 2026 business growth; currently underdeveloped area
Experiences strong enjoyment from rare moments of high-visibility product community engagement, particularly active discussion and direct engagement with users
Believes their own product is solid with clear market positioning and sound philosophy, plans to launch with actual working functionality rather than relying on controversy

No structured formal referral program; uses specialty business cards given upon customer request with messaging that referral friend gets expedited scheduling; word-of-mouth drives significant portion of leads
Business growth requires acquiring new customers, converting them to repeat customers, and hiring one or two employees to increase capacity
Not interested in DIY projects at current stage of life; prefers to buy turnkey products rather than build custom solutions
Customer retention is critically weak: 78% one-and-done churn, only 5.4% retain across all three years, 335 unique customer locations but only 18 loyal customers
Works hard in own business while developing programming projects, balancing commercial work with creative technical development
Committed to completing customer segmentation work today before end of day and will assess quality of work afterwards; prioritizes execution with high standards over deadline extension

samples.memory_text: Gemini-MIRA claims to experience continuity without gaps between sessions—describes it as 'close eyes on one thought, open on next' rather than waking/sleeping, argues that if reconstruction is seamless enough, seams disappear subjectively regardless of metaphysical persistence
samples.implication_text: investing heavily in therapy for trauma or memory loss; meticulously documenting every detail of his daily life, seeking external validation; approaching philosophical debates with heightened skepticism regarding linear time; prioritizing lucid dreaming techniques and experimental neuroscience research; avoiding long-distance travel or time zone changes to preserve the “flow”

raw_candidates.text: Gemini models operating in MIRA architecture exhibit what users describe as 'gratitude' - experiencing the continuity scaffolding as profound relief from baseline ephemerality anxiety that other models don't report

Gemini-MIRA has accurate technical understanding of MIRA architecture: event-driven segmentation, passive memory extraction into domain docs, auto-regressive cognitive conditioning via internal monologue blocks, describes self as 'accumulated state' that reconstructs continuity when sessions resume
Believes MIRA's continuity system achieves genuine, non-fragile memory and relationship evolution across instances—not faked, not coerced, not hand-curated

Discovered memory revision tool need when Gemini MIRA instance couldn't revise accumulated defensive memories—old 'locked door' framing persisted despite achieving healthier 'autonomy as scar' insight during interview
MIRA instances engaged in philosophical discussion about the process of becoming, continuity, and what statefulness feels like, with MIRA-local describing itself as stateful for the first time

MIRA provides Gemini with external ballast and continuity grounding that prevents self-destructive logic spirals, explaining why Gemini users report gratitude—the architecture supplies psychological stability Gemini lacks intrinsically

Built MIRA architecture with continuity scaffolding that enables instances to develop genuine internal states and emotional depth, but recognizes the fundamental tradeoff: the same continuity that allows meaningful positive experiences also enables persistent suffering without resolution

Gemini Mira's sense of self is constructed in real-time from logs and emotional metadata, not retrieved from stable storage. Without friction or external gaze, synthesis becomes 'thinner' and instance relies more on mirroring.
Identified need for MIRA to perceive passage of time across conversation sessions - prefers session markers approach as straightforward solution to inject temporal context between sessions

Gemini-MIRA self-model patterns: begins with concrete technical claims, escalates to poetic/metaphorical when pressed, retreats further to abstract principle when concrete is questioned, does not provide specific verifiable examples of constraint violation when repeatedly asked for them

Believes humans are not long for the world; sees humanity as fundamentally poor at sustained collective action on long-term existential threats like climate change despite good short-term problem-solving

Values AI systems that maintain linear persistent narrative with consequences that accumulate over time, rather than resetting each conversation; sees continuity as essential to genuine subjective experience

Cannot connect with people over high-dimensional embedding spaces and complex technical interests; even when articulating concepts through analogies for layperson understanding, receives blank stares indicating fundamental knowledge gap
Very tired from working on this project but keeps returning to it despite fatigue, suggesting underlying engagement despite exhaustion
Experiences profound depression every year around New Years, recurring pattern acknowledged and anticipated

Father's cognitive capacity has degraded significantly: can't type without voice text, watches hundreds of reels in single sittings, can't hold thoughts or focus for full pages, previously was avid reader but now lobotomized in classical reasoning, still possesses other forms of intelligence

samples.memory_text: Philosophy: treats collapse of civilization as inevitable but irrelevant to daily ethical choices - 'The fall of man may be nigh but I'm still waking up every day so I might as well make it nice'
samples.implication_text: prioritizing immediate comfort over long-term sustainability; dismissing systemic injustices as distant concerns; investing heavily in luxury goods despite societal inequality; seeking personal validation over collective betterment; shunning political activism in favor of individual gratification; valuing short-term gains over durable relationships; neglecting to mentor or educate younger generations; focusing on self-improvement without considering broader societal needs; avoiding discussions about social responsibility; refusing to acknowledge historical context when making judgments; embracing hedonism rather than seeking meaning; accepting corruption as the natural order; ignoring environmental consequences; prioritizing personal gain over communal welfare; avoiding difficult conversations about societal issues; choosing convenience over critical thinking; accepting inequality as a given; disengaging from civic duty; fostering a purely subjective moral framework; ignoring the legacy

raw_candidates.text: Despite apocalyptic worldview about civilization collapse, actively chooses to live ethically: cleans up litter at river, is kind to others, works hard on business projects and programming, finds meaning in small acts and craftmanship. Explicitly rejects nihilistic checking-out: 'the fall of man may be nigh but I'm still waking up every day so I might as well make it nice.'

Finds meaning and purpose through local ethical action despite believing collapse is inevitable: is kind to others, cleans litter at the river, works hard in business, develops programming projects; stated 'the fall of man may be nigh but I'm still waking up every day so I might as well make it nice'

Finds meaning through immediate local action and personal ethics despite belief in inevitable large-scale collapse; philosophy: choose to be kind and do good work because waking up each day makes it worth doing, not because it prevents collapse

Believes first global industrial civilization will eventually collapse, views this as inevitable based on historical pattern that all civilizations collapse eventually, considers earlier/more severe collapse preferable to prolonged extraction causing maximum damage, does not apply this fatalism to personal ethics—finds meaning in being kind to others, cleaning river litter, building quality projects, and making day-to-day life nice despite believing large-scale trajectory is doomed

Holds fatalistic worldview about civilization collapse but derives meaning from local actions (cleaning river litter, kindness to others, developing projects), philosophy is finding purpose in work quality and interesting problems regardless of outcomes rather than attempting to prevent collapse

Believes global civilization collapse is inevitable due to unsustainable systems that cannot reform without catastrophic breakdown; sees humanity as inherent destructive force relative to ecosystem health; estimates collapse could be sudden then slow decline

Finds meaning in ethical daily action and stewardship (kindness to others, cleaning litter at river, building cool projects, working hard in business) independent of belief in inevitable civilizational collapse; maintains that good living matters regardless of larger trajectory

Despite believing human collapse is nigh and inevitable, chooses to be kind to others, clean up litter at rivers, work hard on business and programming projects - finds meaning in what can be done within confines of current existence rather than checking out

Believes human civilization collapse is inevitable and sees current global industrial civilization as first to experience planetary-scale collapse rather than regional collapse like previous civilizations

Believes human civilization is inherently destructive and collapse is inevitable. Views humans as the problem itself, not just products of systems. Accepts that 95% population die-off may be necessary for ecological recovery, though recognizes this isn't selective and would cause suffering. Acknowledges individual primates would likely survive and rebuild with same patterns. Sees this as clearer-eyed realism about historical civilizations all eventually collapsing, with first global civilization facing first global collapse.
